content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description.txt:1-3 claims "AI-powered suggestions," "built-in ad blocker," and "works fully offline," but ContentView.swift (the app's only visible logic) implements just a plain in-memory task list with no ML/AI framework, no ad-blocking/content-blocker extension, and no offline-storage/persistence code.

Pierre: Apple's reviewers actively test marketing claims against the actual app, and unsubstantiated "AI," "ad blocker," or "offline" claims are a common 2.1/2.3 rejection reason since they mislead users. I found no CoreML/CreateML imports, no ContentBlocker extension target, and no persistence (UserDefaults/CoreData/file I/O) to support "works fully offline" — tasks are stored only in `@State` and vanish on relaunch. Either implement these features or rewrite the description to match what TaskTide actually does today.
